name: CoreCivic, Inc.
summary: CoreCivic, Inc. is a private operator of correctional and detention facilities providing residential services to federal, state, and local government agencies. The company owns and manages prisons, immigration detention centers, and residential reentry centers. Key federal customers include ICE, BOP, and USMS. CoreCivic offers flexible capacity solutions, offender programming, and facility management services, allowing governments to expand correctional capacity without large capital commitments or long-term pension obligations.

products.name: Correctional Facility Management
products.description: Ownership and operation of correctional facilities for federal, state, and local government inmates.

products.name: Immigration Detention Services
products.description: Detention and residential services for ICE detainees at owned and managed facilities.

products.name: Residential Reentry Services
products.description: Community-based residential reentry programs supporting offender rehabilitation and reintegration.

products.name: Managed-Only Facility Services
products.description: Management services for government-owned correctional and detention facilities without CoreCivic ownership.

partnerships.description: Intergovernmental service agreements and contracts for detention bed capacity at multiple facilities.
Contracts to house federally convicted inmates at CoreCivic-operated facilities.
Contracts to house pretrial detainees awaiting trial on behalf of the DOJ.
IGSA intermediary enabling ICE contract for Diamondback Correctional Facility operations.
Management contract to house Montana state inmates at CoreCivic-operated Saguaro facility.
